I've got this picture of myself out in the middle of the road, you.

I start imagining everyone judging me, and then I feel this overwhelming pressure, like all eyes are on me, even when I know they probably aren't. It just makes me want to avoid those situations altogether.

patient.query: Um, well, it usually starts when I’m just going about my day. Like, maybe I have to speak in front of people or even just be in a group. I get this tightness in my chest, and my stomach starts churning. And then I kind of notice my thoughts shifting, and that’s when the voice kicks in, amplifying everything. It feels like... like something is building up, like I can’t escape it.

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

Okay, it sounds like these situations – speaking in front of people or even just being in a group – trigger a really distressing experience for you. patient.query: Yeah, it’s like I suddenly become acutely aware of everything—almost too aware. My heart starts racing, and there's this knot in my stomach. Sometimes I just want to zone out and not be present at all, like if I can’t see it, then it won’t hurt me. I feel like I’m on the edge, waiting for something bad to happen. It’s a strange mix of dread and just feeling really small and exposed.

It makes sense that you'd want to feel safe, and that desire to not be present, to feel like "if I can’t see it, then it won’t hurt me," is a way of trying to protect yourself from that feeling of dread and exposure.
